verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचभगवानात्मनात्मानं राम उत्तमकल्पकै: ।सर्वदेवमयं देवमीजेऽथाचार्यवान् मखै: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācabhagavān ātmanātmānaṁrāma uttama-kalpakaiḥsarva-devamayaṁ devamīje ’thācāryavān makhaiḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Thereafter, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Lord Rāmacandra, accepted an ācārya and performed sacrifices [yajñas] with opulent paraphernalia. Thus He Himself worshiped Himself, for He is the Supreme Lord of all demigods.

verses.sanskrit: होत्रेऽददाद् दिशं प्राचीं ब्रह्मणे दक्षिणां प्रभु: ।अध्वर्यवे प्रतीचीं वा उत्तरां सामगाय स: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: hotre ’dadād diśaṁ prācīṁbrahmaṇe dakṣiṇāṁ prabhuḥadhvaryave pratīcīṁ vāuttarāṁ sāmagāya saḥ
verses.text: Lord Rāmacandra gave the entire east to the hotā priest, the entire south to the brahmā priest, the west to the adhvaryu priest, and the north to the udgātā priest, the reciter of the Sāma Veda. In this way, He donated His kingdom.

verses.sanskrit: आचार्याय ददौ शेषां यावती भूस्तदन्तरा ।मन्यमान इदं कृत्‍स्‍नं ब्राह्मणोऽर्हति नि:स्पृह: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: ācāryāya dadau śeṣāṁyāvatī bhūs tad-antarāmanyamāna idaṁ kṛtsnaṁbrāhmaṇo ’rhati niḥspṛhaḥ
verses.text: Thereafter, thinking that because the brāhmaṇas have no material desires they should possess the entire world, Lord Rāmacandra delivered the land between the east, west, north and south to the ācārya.

verses.sanskrit: इत्ययं तदलङ्कारवासोभ्यामवशेषित: ।तथा राज्ञ्यपि वैदेही सौमङ्गल्यावशेषिता ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity ayaṁ tad-alaṅkāra-vāsobhyām avaśeṣitaḥtathā rājñy api vaidehīsaumaṅgalyāvaśeṣitā
verses.text: After thus giving everything in charity to the brāhmaṇas, Lord Rāmacandra retained only His personal garments and ornaments, and similarly the Queen, mother Sītā, was left with only her nose ring, and nothing else.

verses.sanskrit: ते तु ब्राह्मणदेवस्य वात्सल्यं वीक्ष्य संस्तुतम् ।प्रीता: क्लिन्नधियस्तस्मै प्रत्यर्प्येदं बभाषिरे ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: te tu brāhmaṇa-devasyavātsalyaṁ vīkṣya saṁstutamprītāḥ klinna-dhiyas tasmaipratyarpyedaṁ babhāṣire
verses.text: All the brāhmaṇas who were engaged in the various activities of the sacrifice were very pleased with Lord Rāmacandra, who was greatly affectionate and favorable to the brāhmaṇas. Thus with melted hearts they returned all the property received from Him and spoke as follows.

verses.sanskrit: अप्रत्तं नस्त्वया किं नु भगवन् भुवनेश्वर ।यन्नोऽन्तर्हृदयं विश्य तमो हंसि स्वरोचिषा ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: aprattaṁ nas tvayā kiṁ nubhagavan bhuvaneśvarayan no ’ntar-hṛdayaṁ viśyatamo haṁsi sva-rociṣā
verses.text: O Lord, You are the master of the entire universe. What have You not given to us? You have entered the core of our hearts and dissipated the darkness of our ignorance by Your effulgence. This is the supreme gift. We do not need a material donation.

verses.sanskrit: नमो ब्रह्मण्यदेवाय रामायाकुण्ठमेधसे ।उत्तमश्लोकधुर्याय न्यस्तदण्डार्पिताङ्‌घ्रये ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: namo brahmaṇya-devāyarāmāyākuṇṭha-medhaseuttamaśloka-dhuryāyanyasta-daṇḍārpitāṅghraye
verses.text: O Lord, You are the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who have accepted the brāhmaṇas as Your worshipable deity. Your knowledge and memory are never disturbed by anxiety. You are the chief of all famous persons within this world, and Your lotus feet are worshiped by sages who are beyond the jurisdiction of punishment. O Lord Rāmacandra, let us offer our respectful obeisances unto You.

verses.sanskrit: कदाचिल्लोकजिज्ञासुर्गूढो रात्र्यामलक्षित: ।चरन्वाचोऽश‍ृणोद् रामो भार्यामुद्दिश्य कस्यचित् ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: kadācil loka-jijñāsurgūḍho rātryām alakṣitaḥcaran vāco ’śṛṇod rāmobhāryām uddiśya kasyacit
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued: Once while Lord Rāmacandra was walking at night incognito, hiding Himself by a disguise to find out the people’s opinion of Himself, He heard a man speaking unfavorably about His wife, Sītādevī.

verses.sanskrit: नाहं बिभर्मि त्वां दुष्टामसतीं परवेश्मगाम् ।स्त्रैणो हि बिभृयात् सीतां रामो नाहं भजे पुन: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: nāhaṁ bibharmi tvāṁ duṣṭāmasatīṁ para-veśma-gāmstraiṇo hi bibhṛyāt sītāṁrāmo nāhaṁ bhaje punaḥ
verses.text: [Speaking to his unchaste wife, the man said] You go to another man’s house, and therefore you are unchaste and polluted. I shall not maintain you any more. A henpecked husband like Lord Rāma may accept a wife like Sītā, who went to another man’s house, but I am not henpecked like Him, and therefore I shall not accept you again.

verses.sanskrit: इति लोकाद् बहुमुखाद् दुराराध्यादसंविद: ।पत्या भीतेन सा त्यक्ता प्राप्ता प्राचेतसाश्रमम् ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: iti lokād bahu-mukhāddurārādhyād asaṁvidaḥpatyā bhītena sā tyaktāprāptā prācetasāśramam
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Men with a poor fund of knowledge and a heinous character speak nonsensically. Fearing such rascals, Lord Rāmacandra abandoned His wife, Sītādevī, although she was pregnant. Thus Sītādevī went to the āśrama of Vālmīki Muni.

verses.sanskrit: अन्तर्वत्‍न्यागते काले यमौ सा सुषुवे सुतौ ।कुशो लव इति ख्यातौ तयोश्चक्रे क्रिया मुनि: ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: antarvatny āgate kāleyamau sā suṣuve sutaukuśo lava iti khyātautayoś cakre kriyā muniḥ
verses.text: When the time came, the pregnant mother Sītādevī gave birth to twin sons, later celebrated as Lava and Kuśa. The ritualistic ceremonies for their birth were performed by Vālmīki Muni.

verses.sanskrit: अङ्गदश्चित्रकेतुश्च लक्ष्मणस्यात्मजौ स्मृतौ ।तक्ष: पुष्कल इत्यास्तां भरतस्य महीपते ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: aṅgadaś citraketuś calakṣmaṇasyātmajau smṛtautakṣaḥ puṣkala ity āstāṁbharatasya mahīpate
verses.text: O Mahārāja Parīkṣit, Lord Lakṣmaṇa had two sons, named Aṅgada and Citraketu, and Lord Bharata also had two sons, named Takṣa and Puṣkala.

verses.sanskrit: सुबाहु: श्रुतसेनश्च शत्रुघ्नस्य बभूवतु: ।गन्धर्वान् कोटिशो जघ्ने भरतो विजये दिशाम् ॥ १३ ॥तदीयं धनमानीय सर्वं राज्ञे न्यवेदयत् ।शत्रुघ्नश्च मधो: पुत्रं लवणं नाम राक्षसम् ।हत्वा मधुवने चक्रे मथुरां नाम वै पुरीम् ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: subāhuḥ śrutasenaś caśatrughnasya babhūvatuḥgandharvān koṭiśo jaghnebharato vijaye diśām
verses.text: Śatrughna had two sons, named Subāhu and Śrutasena. When Lord Bharata went to conquer all directions, He had to kill many millions of Gandharvas, who are generally pretenders. Taking all their wealth, He offered it to Lord Rāmacandra. Śatrughna also killed a Rākṣasa named Lavaṇa, who was the son of Madhu Rākṣasa. Thus He established in the great forest known as Madhuvana the town known as Mathurā.

verses.sanskrit: मुनौ निक्षिप्य तनयौ सीता भर्त्रा विवासिता ।ध्यायन्ती रामचरणौ विवरं प्रविवेश ह ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: munau nikṣipya tanayausītā bhartrā vivāsitādhyāyantī rāma-caraṇauvivaraṁ praviveśa ha
verses.text: Being forsaken by her husband, Sītādevī entrusted her two sons to the care of Vālmīki Muni. Then, meditating upon the lotus feet of Lord Rāmacandra, she entered into the earth.

verses.sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा भगवान् रामो रुन्धन्नपि धिया शुच: ।स्मरंस्तस्या गुणांस्तांस्तान्नाशक्नोद् रोद्धुमीश्वर: ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tac chrutvā bhagavān rāmorundhann api dhiyā śucaḥsmaraṁs tasyā guṇāṁs tāṁs tānnāśaknod roddhum īśvaraḥ
verses.text: After hearing the news of mother Sītā’s entering the earth, the Supreme Personality of Godhead was certainly aggrieved. Although He is the Supreme Personality of Godhead, upon remembering the exalted qualities of mother Sītā, He could not check His grief in transcendental love.

verses.sanskrit: स्त्रीपुंप्रसङ्ग एताद‍ृक्सर्वत्र त्रासमावह: ।अपीश्वराणां किमुत ग्राम्यस्य गृहचेतस: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: strī-puṁ-prasaṅga etādṛksarvatra trāsam-āvahaḥapīśvarāṇāṁ kim utagrāmyasya gṛha-cetasaḥ
verses.text: The attraction between man and woman, or male and female, always exists everywhere, making everyone always fearful. Such feelings are present even among the controllers like Brahmā and Lord Śiva and is the cause of fear for them, what to speak of others who are attached to household life in this material world.

verses.sanskrit: तत ऊर्ध्वं ब्रह्मचर्यं धार्यन्नजुहोत् प्रभु: ।त्रयोदशाब्दसाहस्रमग्निहोत्रमखण्डितम् ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tata ūrdhvaṁ brahmacaryaṁdhāryann ajuhot prabhuḥtrayodaśābda-sāhasramagnihotram akhaṇḍitam
verses.text: After mother Sītā entered the earth, Lord Rāmacandra observed complete celibacy and performed an uninterrupted Agnihotra-yajña for thirteen thousand years.

verses.sanskrit: स्मरतां हृदि विन्यस्य विद्धं दण्डककण्टकै: ।स्वपादपल्लवं राम आत्मज्योतिरगात् तत: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: smaratāṁ hṛdi vinyasyaviddhaṁ daṇḍaka-kaṇṭakaiḥsva-pāda-pallavaṁ rāmaātma-jyotir agāt tataḥ
verses.text: After completing the sacrifice, Lord Rāmacandra, whose lotus feet were sometimes pierced by thorns when He lived in Daṇḍakāraṇya, placed those lotus feet in the hearts of those who always think of Him. Then He entered His own abode, the Vaikuṇṭha planet beyond the brahmajyoti.

verses.sanskrit: नेदं यशो रघुपते: सुरयाच्ञयात्त-लीलातनोरधिकसाम्यविमुक्तधाम्न: ।रक्षोवधो जलधिबन्धनमस्त्रपूगै:किं तस्य शत्रुहनने कपय: सहाया: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: nedaṁ yaśo raghupateḥ sura-yācñayātta-līlā-tanor adhika-sāmya-vimukta-dhāmnaḥrakṣo-vadho jaladhi-bandhanam astra-pūgaiḥkiṁ tasya śatru-hanane kapayaḥ sahāyāḥ
verses.text: Lord Rāmacandra’s reputation for having killed Rāvaṇa with showers of arrows at the request of the demigods and for having built a bridge over the ocean does not constitute the factual glory of the Supreme Personality of Godhead Lord Rāmacandra, whose spiritual body is always engaged in various pastimes. Lord Rāmacandra has no equal or superior, and therefore He had no need to take help from the monkeys to gain victory over Rāvaṇa.

verses.sanskrit: यस्यामलं नृपसद:सु यशोऽधुनापिगायन्त्यघघ्नमृषयो दिगिभेन्द्रपट्टम् ।तं नाकपालवसुपालकिरीटजुष्ट-पादाम्बुजं रघुपतिं शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: yasyāmalaṁ nṛpa-sadaḥsu yaśo ’dhunāpigāyanty agha-ghnam ṛṣayo dig-ibhendra-paṭṭamtaṁ nākapāla-vasupāla-kirīṭa-juṣṭa-pādāmbujaṁ raghupatiṁ śaraṇaṁ prapadye
verses.text: Lord Rāmacandra’s spotless name and fame, which vanquish all sinful reactions, are celebrated in all directions, like the ornamental cloth of the victorious elephant that conquers all directions. Great saintly persons like Mārkaṇḍeya Ṛṣi still glorify His characteristics in the assemblies of great emperors like Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira. Similarly, all the saintly kings and all the demigods, including Lord Śiva and Lord Brahmā, worship the Lord by bowing down with their helmets. Let me offer my obeisances unto His lotus feet.

verses.sanskrit: स यै: स्पृष्टोऽभिद‍ृष्टो वा संविष्टोऽनुगतोऽपि वा ।कोसलास्ते ययु: स्थानं यत्र गच्छन्ति योगिन: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa yaiḥ spṛṣṭo ’bhidṛṣṭo vāsaṁviṣṭo ’nugato ’pi vākosalās te yayuḥ sthānaṁyatra gacchanti yoginaḥ
verses.text: Lord Rāmacandra returned to His abode, to which bhakti-yogīs are promoted. This is the place to which all the inhabitants of Ayodhyā went after they served the Lord in His manifest pastimes by offering Him obeisances, touching His lotus feet, fully observing Him as a fatherlike King, sitting or lying down with Him like equals, or even just accompanying Him.

verses.sanskrit: पुरुषो रामचरितं श्रवणैरुपधारयन् ।आनृशंस्यपरो राजन् कर्मबन्धैर्विमुच्यते ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: puruṣo rāma-caritaṁśravaṇair upadhārayanānṛśaṁsya-paro rājankarma-bandhair vimucyate
verses.text: O King Parīkṣit, anyone who aurally receives the narrations concerning the characteristics of Lord Rāmacandra’s pastimes will ultimately be freed from the disease of envy and thus be liberated from the bondage of fruitive activities.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीराजोवाचकथं स भगवान् रामो भ्रातृन् वा स्वयमात्मन: ।तस्मिन् वा तेऽन्ववर्तन्त प्रजा: पौराश्च ईश्वरे ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-rājovācakathaṁ sa bhagavān rāmobhrātṝn vā svayam ātmanaḥtasmin vā te ’nvavartantaprajāḥ paurāś ca īśvare
verses.text: Mahārāja Parīkṣit inquired from Śukadeva Gosvāmī: How did the Lord conduct Himself, and how did He behave in relationship with His brothers, who were expansions of His own self? And how did His brothers and the inhabitants of Ayodhyā treat Him?

verses.sanskrit: श्रीबादरायणिरुवाचअथादिशद् दिग्विजये भ्रातृंस्त्रिभुवनेश्वर: ।आत्मानं दर्शयन् स्वानां पुरीमैक्षत सानुग: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bādarāyaṇir uvācaathādiśad dig-vijayebhrātṝṁs tri-bhuvaneśvaraḥātmānaṁ darśayan svānāṁpurīm aikṣata sānugaḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī replied: After accepting the throne of the government by the fervent request of His younger brother Bharata, Lord Rāmacandra ordered His younger brothers to go out and conquer the entire world, while He personally remained in the capital to give audience to all the citizens and residents of the palace and supervise the governmental affairs with His other assistants.

verses.sanskrit: आसिक्तमार्गां गन्धोदै: करिणां मदशीकरै: ।स्वामिनं प्राप्तमालोक्य मत्तां वा सुतरामिव ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: āsikta-mārgāṁ gandhodaiḥkariṇāṁ mada-śīkaraiḥsvāminaṁ prāptam ālokyamattāṁ vā sutarām iva
verses.text: During the reign of Lord Rāmacandra, the streets of the capital, Ayodhyā, were sprinkled with perfumed water and drops of perfumed liquor, thrown about by elephants from their trunks. When the citizens saw the Lord personally supervising the affairs of the city in such opulence, they appreciated this opulence very much.

verses.sanskrit: प्रासादगोपुरसभाचैत्यदेवगृहादिषु ।विन्यस्तहेमकलशै: पताकाभिश्च मण्डिताम् ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: prāsāda-gopura-sabhā-caitya-deva-gṛhādiṣuvinyasta-hema-kalaśaiḥpatākābhiś ca maṇḍitām
verses.text: The palaces, the palace gates, the assembly houses, the platforms for meeting places, the temples and all such places were decorated with golden waterpots and bedecked with various types of flags.

verses.sanskrit: पूगै: सवृन्तै रम्भाभि: पट्टिकाभि: सुवाससाम् ।आदर्शैरंशुकै: स्रग्भि: कृतकौतुकतोरणाम् ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: pūgaiḥ savṛntai rambhābhiḥpaṭṭikābhiḥ suvāsasāmādarśair aṁśukaiḥ sragbhiḥkṛta-kautuka-toraṇām
verses.text: Wherever Lord Rāmacandra visited, auspicious welcome gates were constructed, with banana trees and betel-nut trees, full of flowers and fruits. The gates were decorated with various flags made of colorful cloth and with tapestries, mirrors and garlands.

verses.sanskrit: तमुपेयुस्तत्र तत्र पौरा अर्हणपाणय: ।आशिषो युयुजुर्देव पाहीमां प्राक्त्वयोद्‍धृताम् ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam upeyus tatra tatrapaurā arhaṇa-pāṇayaḥāśiṣo yuyujur devapāhīmāṁ prāk tvayoddhṛtām
verses.text: Wherever Lord Rāmacandra visited, the people approached Him with paraphernalia of worship and begged the Lord’s blessings. “O Lord,” they said, “as You rescued the earth from the bottom of the sea in Your incarnation as a boar, may You now maintain it. Thus we beg Your blessings.”

verses.sanskrit: तत: प्रजा वीक्ष्य पतिं चिरागतंदिद‍ृक्षयोत्सृष्टगृहा: स्त्रियो नरा: ।आरुह्य हर्म्याण्यरविन्दलोचन-मतृप्तनेत्रा: कुसुमैरवाकिरन् ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: tataḥ prajā vīkṣya patiṁ cirāgataṁdidṛkṣayotsṛṣṭa-gṛhāḥ striyo narāḥāruhya harmyāṇy aravinda-locanamatṛpta-netrāḥ kusumair avākiran
verses.text: Thereafter, not having seen the Lord for a long time, the citizens, both men and women, being very eager to see Him, left their homes and got up on the roofs of the palaces. Being incompletely satiated with seeing the face of the lotus-eyed Lord Rāmacandra, they showered flowers upon Him.

verses.sanskrit: अथ प्रविष्ट: स्वगृहं जुष्टं स्वै: पूर्वराजभि: ।अनन्ताखिलकोषाढ्यमनर्घ्योरुपरिच्छदम् ॥ ३१ ॥विद्रुमोदुम्बरद्वारैर्वैदूर्यस्तम्भपङ्क्तिभि: ।स्थलैर्मारकतै: स्वच्छैर्भ्राजत्स्फटिकभित्तिभि: ॥ ३२ ॥चित्रस्रग्भि: पट्टिकाभिर्वासोमणिगणांशुकै: ।मुक्ताफलैश्चिदुल्लासै: कान्तकामोपपत्तिभि: ॥ ३३ ॥धूपदीपै: सुरभिभिर्मण्डितं पुष्पमण्डनै: ।स्त्रीपुम्भि: सुरसङ्काशैर्जुष्टं भूषणभूषणै: ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: atha praviṣṭaḥ sva-gṛhaṁjuṣṭaṁ svaiḥ pūrva-rājabhiḥanantākhila-koṣāḍhyamanarghyoruparicchadam
verses.text: Thereafter, Lord Rāmacandra entered the palace of His forefathers. Within the palace were various treasures and valuable wardrobes. The sitting places on the two sides of the entrance door were made of coral, the yards were surrounded by pillars of vaidūrya-maṇi, the floor was made of highly polished marakata-maṇi, and the foundation was made of marble. The entire palace was decorated with flags and garlands and bedecked with valuable stones, shining with a celestial effulgence. The palace was fully decorated with pearls and surrounded by lamps and incense. The men and women within the palace all resembled demigods and were decorated with various ornaments, which seemed beautiful because of being placed on their bodies.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिन्स भगवान् राम: स्‍निग्धया प्रिययेष्टया ।रेमे स्वारामधीराणामृषभ: सीतया किल ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmin sa bhagavān rāmaḥsnigdhayā priyayeṣṭayāreme svārāma-dhīrāṇāmṛṣabhaḥ sītayā kila
verses.text: Lord Rāmacandra, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, chief of the best learned scholars, resided in that palace with His pleasure potency, mother Sītā, and enjoyed complete peace.

verses.sanskrit: बुभुजे च यथाकालं कामान् धर्ममपीडयन् ।वर्षपूगान् बहून् नृणामभिध्याताङ्‌घ्रिपल्लव: ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: bubhuje ca yathā-kālaṁkāmān dharmam apīḍayanvarṣa-pūgān bahūn nṝṇāmabhidhyātāṅghri-pallavaḥ
verses.text: Without transgressing the religious principles, Lord Rāmacandra, whose lotus feet are worshiped by devotees in meditation, enjoyed with all the paraphernalia of transcendental pleasure for as long as needed.
